text.title: Kano Prioritization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Kano Prioritization application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each feature MUST be tested with both a functional ('how would you feel if X were present?') and a dysfunctional ('how would you feel if X were absent?') question. Single-question surveys produce no Kano category.
rule.rationale: Kano model (Kano, 1984) — anchors Kano Prioritization rule `two-question-survey-form`.

rule.statement: Each feature's category MUST be assigned by the canonical Kano response-pair table (M, P, A, I, R, Q). Manual overrides require a written rationale.
rule.rationale: Kano methodology — anchors Kano Prioritization rule `category-assignment-table`.

rule.statement: Per segment, ≥30 valid responses are required before category is finalised. Smaller samples produce noisy classifications.
rule.rationale: Survey-statistics research — anchors Kano Prioritization rule `minimum-sample-thirty`.

rule.statement: Category labels expire after 90 days. Customer expectations shift; stale labels mislead.
rule.rationale: Expectation-shift research — anchors Kano Prioritization rule `revisit-cadence-90d`.

rule.statement: Reverse (R) features MUST be flagged with severity (high if MRR-impacting). Reverse features are removal candidates, not enhancement candidates.
rule.rationale: Anti-feature research — anchors Kano Prioritization rule `reverse-features-flagged`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
